name: Ascension
description_short: Ascension Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union serving five Louisiana parishes with personal loans, vehicle financing, home equity products, and deposit accounts.
description_long:
Ascension Credit Union operates as a community-owned financial cooperative serving Ascension, Assumption, Iberville, Livingston, and St. James Parishes in Louisiana. As a certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI), the organization operates on a not-for-profit, member-driven model with the stated mission of "People Helping People." The institution is led by President and CEO Lloyd Cockerham and emphasizes reinvestment into local communities.

The credit union offers a range of consumer financial products including personal loans (marketed as "We Say YES!" loans designed for borrowers rejected by traditional banks), vehicle refinancing through their "Switch & Ditch" program, home equity loans and HELOCs, boat and recreational vehicle financing, and standard deposit products. Members gain access to online and mobile banking, appointment scheduling, competitive rates, and lower fees compared to traditional banks. The organization actively promotes membership with quick enrollment and advertises exclusive member benefits.

Ascension Credit Union distinguishes itself through its explicit positioning as an alternative to large banks, its CDFI certification, and its community reinvestment focus. The website emphasizes member testimonials, personalized service, and the flexibility to approve loans that traditional banks decline. Their marketing campaigns like "Switch & Ditch" specifically target borrowers with high-rate auto loans and other vehicles.

As a regional credit union, Ascension is limited to specific Louisiana parishes, which restricts membership availability compared to national banks or online-only institutions. Like all credit unions, membership typically requires meeting eligibility criteria. While the website presents strong testimonials and competitive positioning, actual rate competitiveness and loan approval standards cannot be verified from the website alone.
services: Personal loans ("We Say YES!" product line)
Auto loan refinancing through "Switch & Ditch" program
RV, boat, motorcycle, ATV, tractor, and watercraft financing
Online banking platform with bill pay and account management
Membership account opening with deposit requirements
Financial education resources and blog content
Annual member meetings and voting rights
pros: Certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) with explicit community reinvestment focus
Marketed specifically to borrowers declined by traditional banks with "We Say YES!" lending philosophy
Offers vehicle refinancing program ("Switch & Ditch") across multiple vehicle types including autos, RVs, boats, motorcycles, ATVs
Not-for-profit, member-owned structure means profits reinvested rather than distributed to shareholders
Free mobile app for 24/7 account access and management
Home equity and HELOC products with flexible terms for renovations and debt consolidation
Fast loan approval with testimonials indicating same-day vehicle purchase financing
cons: Membership limited to five Louisiana parishes (Ascension, Assumption, Iberville, Livingston, St. James) — geographic restriction excludes most of the country
No specific interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules disclosed on website — requires application or contact for pricing details
No indication of online membership application; website suggests in-person or appointment-based enrollment
Limited digital presence for loan applications compared to online-first lenders; most processes require scheduling appointments
No transparent disclosure of loan approval criteria or credit score requirements despite marketing to borrowers declined elsewhere
best_for: Louisiana residents in the five-parish service area seeking an alternative to traditional banks for personal or vehicle loans
Borrowers with lower credit scores or previous bank rejections who qualify for CDFI lending criteria
Vehicle owners seeking refinancing to lower monthly payments on auto, RV, boat, or recreational vehicle loans
Homeowners in the service area with home equity seeking flexible HELOC or home equity loan products
diagnosis: Ascension Credit Union is best for Louisiana residents in its five-parish service area who seek community-focused, not-for-profit banking and are interested in personal loans, vehicle refinancing, or home equity products. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation — membership is restricted to specific parishes, making this institution inaccessible to the vast majority of U.S. consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
